original: World's first mass vaccine program against Malaria begins in Yaoundé, Cameroon, after successful trials of RTS,S vaccine showed it saved one in three lives
Temperature rises 49°F (9°C) in 2 minutes in Spearfish, South Dakota
Australian surfer Laura Enever sets world record riding the largest-ever wave by a woman at 43.6ft (13.3m) on Oahu's North Shore, Hawaii
Joel Embiidsets NBA 76ers franchise record with 70 points in a single game during 133-123 win over visiting San Antonio Spurs, in Philadelphia; previous record of 68 set byWilt Chamberlainin 1967
Kobe Bryantscores 81 points in a 122-104 Lakers victory over the Toronto Raptors; second-highest game total in NBA history, behind only Wilt Chamberlain's 100-point game (1962)
Apple's seminal ad for the newMacintoshcomputer, directed byRidley Scott, loosely based onGeorge Orwell's "1984", plays during the Super Bowl
World's largest cheese (15,723 kg) is manufactured in Wisconsin for the New York World's Fair
Water vapour is detected on the dwarf planet Ceres
Great comet of 1471 (C/1471 Y1) becomes the closest comet in modern times, coming within 10 million kilometers of Earth
"Cleopatra's Needle", a 3,500-year-old Ancient Egyptian obelisk is erected in Central Park, New York
75% of North America is covered by snow
China's northernmost city, Mohe, records record low temperature of -53C (-63F)
First commercial Boeing 747 flight, Pan American World Airways flies from New York City to London in 6½ hours

Katie Mulcahey is arrested for lighting a cigarette, violating the one-day-old "Sullivan Ordinance" banning women from smoking in public, and is fined $5. Appearing before the judge, she states, “I’ve got as much right to smoke as you have. I never heard of this new law, and I don’t want to hear about it. No man shall dictate to me.”
Willa Brown, American air pioneer (NAAA) and civil rights activist (1st African American woman to hold a pilot's license), born in Glasgow, Kentucky (d. 1992)
Hilbert van de Thumb becomes European Allround speedskating champion
Battle of Rorke's Drift: British garrison of 150 holds off 3,000-4,000 Zulu warriors. Eleven Victoria Crosses and several other decorations are awarded to the defenders
Annette Kennedy of SUNY sets women's college basketball record with 70 points in 116-21 victory over Pratt Institute at Purchase, New York
George ForemanTKOsJoe Frazierin 2 rounds to win WBC & WBA heavyweight boxing titles in Kingston, Jamaica; Frazier knocked down 3 times in both 1st & 2nd rounds
Thomas David Jones, United States astronaut (STS 59, 68, 80, sk: 98), born in Baltimore, Maryland
NASA's Apollo 5: First unmanned test flight of Apollo Lunar Module is launched in earth orbit by Saturn 1B.
Space Shuttle STS-42 (Discovery 15) launches into space
Space shuttle Atlantis successfully returns to Earth
Lev Landau, Russian physicist (1962 Nobel Prize for Physics for superfluidity theory), born in Baku, Russian Empire (d. 1968)
Robert E. Howard, American pulp fiction author (Conan the Barbarian), born in Peaster, Texas (d. 1936)
Super Bowl XVIII, Tampa Stadium, Tampa, FL: LA Raiders beat Washington Redskins, 38-9; MVP: Marcus Allen, Los Angeles, RB
Super Bowl XXIII, Joe Robbie Stadium, Miami, FL: San Francisco 49ers beat Cincinnati Bengals, 20-16; MVP:Jerry Rice, SF, WR
Linda Blair, Americanactress(Exorcist, Chained Heat, Savage St), born in St Louis, Missouri
John Hurt, English actor (Elephant Man, Alien, Midnight Express), born in Chesterfield (d. 2017)

Buddy Hollymakes his last recordings in his NYC apartment, alone with an acoustic guitar; he tapes five songs including "Peggy Sue" and "Crying, Waiting, Hoping", which were embellished, overdubbed, and released posthumously by Coral Records
"Lady Soul" 14th studio album byAretha Franklinis released (Billboard Album of the Year, 1968)
Defending championMike Tysonbeats former titleholderLarry Holmesby TKO in round 4 at Convention Centre, Atlantic City to retain his undisputed heavyweight boxing title
Blizzard in NJ, as 334 attend Devils-Flame NHL game, NJ wins 7-5
-30°F (-34°C), Mountain Lake Bio Station, Virginia (state record)
66.34 cm (26.12") precipitation over 24 hour period in Hoegees Camp, California (state record)
Winter storm conditions strand 500 motorists for 24 hours in Somerset and Bedford counties, about 80 miles southeast of Pittsburgh, on the Pennsylvania Turnpike
Chile declares a State of Emergency and requests international assistance as wildfires rage out of control
Chinese Year of the Rabbit begins with China marking the largest celebration of the New Year since 2020, after Covid-zero restrictions lifted
Military triumph of Wak Jalam Chan (Lady Six Sky), female ruler of the Maya city of Sa’aal (Naranjo) as depicted on Stela 24 Naranjo
Ashantis defeat British forces in the Gold Coast
Johan Koss skates a world record 5 km in 6:38.77
Alan J. Heeger, American chemist, 2000 Nobel Prize for Chemistry for discovery and development of conductive polymers, born in Sioux City, Iowa
STS 89 (Endeavour 12) launches into orbit
Orbiting Solar Observatory 5 launched into earth orbit
US launches TIROS 9 weather satellite
Earth Resources Technology Satellite Landsat 2 is launched
Cold wave damages 90% of Florida's citrus crop
NHL's Minnesota franchise selects the nickname Wild
Paul Pender defeats Sugar Ray Robinson for the middleweight boxing title
J.C. Tremblay, Canadian ice hockey defenseman (Stanley Cup x 5 Montreal Canadiens; 7 x NHL All Star), born in Bagotville, Quebec (d. 1994)
Shinichi "Sonny" Chiba [Sadaho Maeda], Japanese martial artist and actor (The Street Fighter; Kill Bill: Volume 1), born in Fukuoka, Japan (d. 2021)
Northern Calloway, American actor (David on "Sesame Street"), born in New York City (d. 1990)
Karen Moe, American swimmer (Olympic gold 200m butterfly 1972), born in Del Monte, Philippines
Charles White, American College Football Hall of Fame running back (Heisman Trophy 1979, USC; Pro Bowl 1987, Cleveland Browns, LA Rams), born in Los Angeles, California (d. 2023)
Steve Perry, American rock vocalist (Journey, 1977-87; 95-97 - "Don't Stop Believin'"; "Open Arms"), born in Hanford, California
Fidel Castrois ejected from a Winter League baseball game after hitting a batter
Roy CampanellaandStan Musialelected to baseball Hall of Fame
Robert Tappan Morris is convicted of releasing the 1988 Internet worm, or Morris worm, one of the oldest computer worms distributed via the Internet
Evo Moralesis inaugurated as President of Bolivia, becoming the country's first indigenous president
The British government launches pro-vaccine campaign
